rulings.Case Title: GEORGEW H FRANKLIN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDENTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 7, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 22. Defendant Lehigh Hanson, Inc.¿s motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiff(s)¿s request for jurisdictional discovery is denied. Plaintiff(s) failed to make any showing of the second element of the alter ego doctrine ¿ i.e. ¿the facts are such that an adherence to the fiction of the separate existence of the corporation would, under the particular circumstances, sanction a fraud or promote injustice¿ (9 Witkin, Summary of California Law (10th ed. 2005) ¿Corporations¿ §10, quoting Minifie v. Rowley (1921) 187 Cal. 481, 487)) ¿ or that any of the jurisdictional discovery sought to be taken will produce evidence to prove that element. (See also SonoraDiamond Corp. v. Superior Court (2000) 83 Cal. App. 4th 523, 539 (alter ego doctrine not a grounds to impose personal jurisdiction where there was no evidence of any wrongdoing by either the subsidiary or the parent or any evidence of injustice flowing from the recognition of the subsidiary¿s separate corporate identity)). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STEVE BRINKMAN, AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.****TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CLARA***
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Amy C.Yi; Declaration Of Hans C. Ruschke
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 7, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 15. Defendant Lehigh Hanson, Inc.¿s motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiff(s)¿s request for jurisdictional discovery is denied. Plaintiff(s) failed to make any showing of the second element of the alter ego doctrine ¿ i.e. ¿the facts are such that an adherence to the fiction of the separate existence of the corporation would, under the particular circumstances, sanction a fraud or promote injustice¿ (9 Witkin, Summary of California Law (10th ed. 2005) ¿Corporations¿ §10, quoting Minifie v. Rowley (1921) 187 Cal. 481, 487)) ¿ or that any of the jurisdictional discovery sought to be taken will produce evidence to prove that element. (See also SonoraDiamond Corp. v. Superior Court (2000) 83 Cal. App. 4th 523, 539 (alter ego doctrine not a grounds to impose personal jurisdiction where there was no evidence of any wrongdoing by either the subsidiary or the parent or any evidence of injustice flowing from the recognition of the subsidiary¿s separate corporate identity)). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LOWELL R. HOELMER et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 7, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 20. Defendant Groeniger & Co.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LOWELL R. HOELMER et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 7, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 20. Defendant Groeniger & Co.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ complaint is off calendar as moot due to the tentative ruling on the demurrer. As guidance for the parties in the future, in the event that plaintiffs file an amended complaint meeting the requirements of Bockrath and re-alleging their allegations of punitive damages and agency, the court is likely to find that the punitive damages and agency allegations are sufficient to withstand a motion to strike. = (220/HEK)
